tools.name: grep
tools.description: Search file contents using ripgrep for function/class/variable definitions, imports, error messages, or any text pattern in code. Use this for content search. For filename search, use glob instead. For directory structure, use tree instead.

pattern.description: Search pattern (regex by default, use --literal for exact text)

type.description: File type filter: go, py, ts, js, etc. (repeatable, use without dots)

literal.description: Treat pattern as literal text, not regex

context.description: Show N lines of context around each match

tools.name: glob
tools.description: Search for files by name pattern using fd. Use this to find files by extension or name. For searching file contents, use grep instead. For directory structure, use tree instead.

type.description: Type filter: f=files only, d=directories only

tools.name: tree
tools.description: Display directory structure as a tree view. Use this to understand project layout and organization. For searching file contents, use grep instead. For searching filenames, use glob instead.

tools.name: check
tools.description: Verify that required dependencies (ripgrep, fd, tree) are installed and accessible. Use this before running search commands to ensure all tools are available.

messages.content: Hi, I'm new to this project and feeling a bit lost. I want to understand how the codebase is organized - like what folders and files are here and how they relate to each other. Could you help me get oriented with the overall structure?
I'd be happy to help you get oriented! Let me show you the overall structure of this project.

{"status": "success", "tool": "tree", "path": ".", "depth": 3, "size": false, "tree": [{"type": "directory", "name": "src", "children": [{"type": "directory", "name": "components", "children": [{"type": "file", "name": "Button.tsx"}, {"type": "file", "name": "Card.tsx"}, {"type": "file", "name": "Input.tsx"}]}, {"type": "directory", "name": "utils", "children": [{"type": "file", "name": "helpers.ts"}, {"type": "file", "name": "api.ts"}]}, {"type": "file", "name": "index.ts"}]}, {"type": "directory", "name": "tests", "children": [{"type": "file", "name": "app.test.ts"}, {"type": "file", "name": "utils.test.ts"}]}, {"type": "file", "name": "package.json"}, {"type": "file", "name": "tsconfig.json"}, {"type": "file", "name": "README.md"}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-tree

messages.content:
Here's a quick overview of your project structure:

## Project Layout

```
├── src/                    # Main source code
│   ├── components/         # Reusable UI components
│   │   ├── Button.tsx
│   │   ├── Card.tsx
│   │   └── Input.tsx
│   ├── utils/              # Helper functions and API logic
│   │   ├── helpers.ts
│   │   └── api.ts
│   └── index.ts            # Entry point
├── tests/                  # Test files
│   ├── app.test.ts
│   └── utils.test.ts
├── package.json            # Project dependencies & scripts
├── tsconfig.json           # TypeScript configuration
└── README.md               # Documentation
```

## How it fits together

- **`src/components/`** — Contains your UI building blocks (Button, Card, Input). These are likely React components since they use the `.tsx` extension.
- **`src/utils/`** — Contains shared logic: `helpers.ts` for general utility functions, and `api.ts` for API/network-related code.
- **`src/index.ts`** — The main entry point that likely brings everything together.
- **`tests/`** — Unit tests for the app and utilities.
- **Root config files** — `package.json` manages dependencies, and `tsconfig.json` configures TypeScript.

This looks like a TypeScript/React project. Does this help you get oriented? Let me know if you'd like me to dig into any specific part!
Thanks for showing me the structure! Now I'm trying to find the files that handle the API functionality - could you help me locate those? I want to understand how the app makes network requests.
Based on the structure I just showed you, the API logic is likely in `src/utils/api.ts`. Let me take a look at that file:
{"status": "success", "tool": "glob", "pattern": "**/api.ts", "results": ["src/api.ts", "src/routes/api.ts", "src/api/v1/api.ts", "lib/api.ts", "api/api.ts"], "count": 5}
messages.name: skill-tools-glob

messages.content: Interesting! There are actually multiple `api.ts` files in the project. Let me find the one that handles the actual network requests by searching for common HTTP methods:
{"path": "src/services/api.ts", "line": 3, "content": "import axios from 'axios';", "context": {"before": [], "after": ["export const apiClient = axios.create({"]}}
messages.name: skill-tools-grep

messages.content: Found it! The API functionality is in `src/services/api.ts`. Let me show you its contents:

{"status": "success", "tool": "grep", "matches": 15, "pattern": ".", "path": "src/services/api.ts", "context": 10, "results": [{"line": 1, "content": "import { fetch, post, get } from './http-client';", "match": "import { fetch, post, get } from './http-client';"}, {"line": 2, "content": "import { API_CONFIG, endpoints } from '../config/api-config';", "match": "import { API_CONFIG, endpoints } from '../config/api-config';"}, {"line": 3, "content": "", "match": ""}, {"line": 4, "content": "const BASE_URL = API_CONFIG.baseUrl;", "match": "const BASE_URL = API_CONFIG.baseUrl;"}, {"line": 5, "content": "", "match": ""}, {"line": 6, "content": "export async function fetchUserData(userId: string) {", "match": "export async function fetchUserData(userId: string) {"}, {"line": 7, "content": "  const url = `${BASE_URL}${endpoints.users}/${userId}`;", "match": "  const url = `${BASE_URL}${endpoints.users}/${userId}`;"}, {"line": 8, "content": "  return fetch(url, { method: 'GET' });", "match": "  return fetch(url, { method: 'GET' });"}, {"line": 9, "content": "}", "match": "}"}, {"line": 10, "content": "", "match": ""}, {"line": 11, "content": "export async function createUser(data: UserPayload) {", "match": "export async function createUser(data: UserPayload) {"}, {"line": 12, "content": "  const url = `${BASE_URL}${endpoints.users}`;", "match": "  const url = `${BASE_URL}${endpoints.users}`;"}, {"line": 13, "content": "  return post(url, data);", "match": "  return post(url, data);"}, {"line": 14, "content": "}", "match": "}"}, {"line": 15, "content": "", "match": ""}]}
messages.name: skill-tools-grep

messages.content: That helps a lot! I can see the API file uses functions like fetch and post from an http-client. Could you show me what's actually in that http-client.ts file? I want to see how those functions are implemented and how they handle the network requests.
{"status": "success", "tool": "glob", "pattern": "**/http-client.ts", "matches": ["src/http-client.ts", "src/lib/http-client.ts", "tests/http-client.test.ts"]}
messages.name: skill-tools-glob

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "grep", "pattern": ".", "path": "src/http-client.ts", "matches": 100, "max_reached": true, "context": 15}
messages.name: skill-tools-grep

messages.content: The file has a lot of content. Let me get a better view by looking at the structure and key parts:

{"status": "success", "matches": [{"line_number": 12, "line": "export async function fetchWithRetry(url: string, options?: RequestInit): Promise<Response> {", "context": {"before": ["import { HttpError } from './errors';", "import { getConfig } from './config';", "", "const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 30000;"], "after": ["  const config = getConfig();", "  const maxRetries = config.maxRetries ?? 3;", "  // implementation...", "  return response;", "}"]}}, {"line_number": 45, "line": "export function createClient(baseUrl: string): HttpClient {", "context": {"before": ["export interface HttpClient {", "  get<T>(path: string): Promise<T>;", "  post<T>(path: string, data: unknown): Promise<T>;", "}"], "after": ["  const client = new HttpClientImpl(baseUrl);", "  return client;", "}"]}}, {"line_number": 78, "line": "export async function request<T>(config: RequestConfig): Promise<T> {", "context": {"before": ["import { validateRequest } from './validation';", ""], "after": ["  const response = await executeRequest(config);", "  return response.data as T;", "}"]}}], "total_matches": 3, "pattern": "^export (async )?function", "path": "src/http-client.ts"}
messages.name: skill-tools-grep

messages.content: Let me also find those simpler `fetch` and `post` functions that are being exported:

{"status": "success", "tool": "grep", "matches": [{"line": 5, "content": "export const fetch = async (url: string, options?: RequestInit) => {"}, {"line": 12, "content": "export function post(url: string, data: any): Promise<Response> {"}, {"line": 23, "content": "export const get = (url: string) => fetch(url, { method: 'GET' });"}], "total_matches": 3, "path": "src/http-client.ts", "pattern": "^export (const|function) (fetch|post|get)"}
messages.name: skill-tools-grep

messages.content: Wow, you've shown me a lot of useful tools! I saw you use different commands to explore the code - like the tree view, searching for files, and finding content inside files. Before I wrap up, could we quickly check that all those tools are actually working properly? I want to make sure everything is set up correctly on my end.
Great idea! Let me verify that all the tools are properly set up:
{"status": "success", "tool": "check", "dependencies": {"ripgrep": "available", "fd": "available", "tree": "available"}, "all_dependencies_present": true}
messages.name: skill-tools-check

messages.content:
All tools are working properly! Here's the check result:

## ✅ Tool Status

| Tool | Status |
|------|--------|
| **ripgrep** (used by grep) | Available |
| **fd** (used by glob) | Available |
| **tree** | Available |

Everything is set up correctly on your end. You're all set to explore the codebase! 

If you have any more questions as you dive into the project, feel free to ask. Happy coding!
That's great to hear! Thanks for checking everything for me. I'm all set now.
You're welcome! Enjoy exploring the project, and good luck with your work here! 👋

```
├── tests/                  # Test files

├── package.json            # Project dependencies & scripts
├── tsconfig.json           # TypeScript configuration
└── README.md               # Documentation
```

## How it fits together

- **`src/components/`** — Contains your UI building blocks (Button, Card, Input). These are likely React components since they use the `.tsx` extension.
- **`src/utils/`** — Contains shared logic: `helpers.ts` for general utility functions, and `api.ts` for API/network-related code.
- **`src/index.ts`** — The main entry point that likely brings everything together.
- **`tests/`** — Unit tests for the app and utilities.
- **Root config files** — `package.json` manages dependencies, and `tsconfig.json` configures TypeScript.
